---
title: ""
---

<h1 class="hero-title">sagui <img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/COINtoolbox/brand-assets/main/logo/COIN_2024_Logo.svg" width="120" alt="COIN logo"></h1>

[![GitHub](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-sagui-0f4c5c?logo=github)](https://github.com/RafaelSdeSouza/sagui)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-0f4c5c)](LICENSE)
[![COIN](https://img.shields.io/badge/COIN-Toolbox-c89b3c)](https://github.com/COINtoolbox)

## Overview

**sagui** is a photometric SED-based segmentation package for IFU data cubes. It is designed to identify spatial regions using a photometric representation of the cube, apply a starlet-based mask, cluster valid spaxels into regions, and extract integrated SEDs with propagated uncertainties for each region.

In contrast to a purely spectral segmentation workflow, **sagui** is centered on:

- photometric preconditioning of the cube
- multiscale masking through starlet reconstruction
- segmentation of valid regions
- integrated region SEDs and uncertainties
- region-level property maps for downstream SED fitting

> Part of the **COIN Toolbox**, with a focus on concise, community-oriented tools for astronomical data analysis.

## Core Workflow

The intended workflow is:

1. collapse the IFU cube into a photometric summary image
2. build a starlet mask from the collapsed image
3. segment the masked cube into regions
4. extract integrated SEDs and uncertainties for each region
5. project derived region properties back onto the image plane

The main user-facing functions are:

- `segment_regions()`
- `build_starlet_mask()`
- `extract_region_sed()`
- `summarize_region_sed()`
- `plot_region_map()`
- `plot_region_property_map()`
- `plot_region_property_raster()`

## Installation

Install from GitHub:

```r
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("RafaelSdeSouza/sagui")
library(sagui)
```

If you want the faster clustering backend, also install the optional acceleration packages:

```r
install.packages("fastcluster")
install.packages("torch")
```

## Usage

### Segment an IFU Cube

```r
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(sagui)
  library(FITSio)
})

x <- FITSio::readFITS("manga-8135-12701-LOGCUBE.fits")

seg <- segment_regions(
  input = x,
  Ncomp = 30,
  use_starlet_mask = TRUE,
  starlet_J = 5,
  starlet_scales = 2:5
)

plot_region_map(seg, palette = "magma")
```

### Extract Integrated SEDs

```r
sed <- extract_region_sed(
  cube = x,
  labels = seg$cluster_map,
  band_values = FITSio::axVec(3, x$axDat)
)

head(sed$flux_long)
```

### Plot a Region Property

```r
plot_region_property_map(
  seg,
  values = setNames(sed$flux_wide$n_pix, sed$flux_wide$region),
  value_label = "Pixels per region"
)
```

## Package Logic

The segmentation stage and the SED summary stage are intentionally separated:

- clustering operates on scaled per-spaxel vectors
- region summaries are based on integrated SEDs, not median representative spectra

This makes **sagui** better aligned with workflows where the final product is a region-integrated SED plus uncertainty for fitting or physical interpretation.

## Dependencies

- **FITSio** for FITS cube handling
- **dplyr**, **tidyr**, **tibble** for table reshaping
- **ggplot2**, **viridis**, **sf** for visualization
- **matrixStats** and **Matrix** for efficient numerical operations
- **fastcluster** and **torch** as optional clustering accelerators

## References

1. Bundy, Kevin, et al. (2015). *Overview of the SDSS-IV MaNGA Survey: Mapping Nearby Galaxies at Apache Point Observatory.* The Astrophysical Journal, 798(1), 7. <https://doi.org/10.1088/0004-637X/798/1/7>
2. de Souza, R. S., et al. (2025). *CAPIVARA: A spectral-based segmentation method for IFU data cubes.* Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 539(4), 3166-3179. <https://doi.org/10.1093/mnras/staf688>
3. Starck, J.-L., Murtagh, F., and Fadili, J. (2015). *Sparse Image and Signal Processing: Wavelets, Curvelets, Morphological Diversity.* Cambridge University Press.
